Alex Jones 2/12/2024 1.0 Updated distribution order display types to accurately reflect alphanumeric values EX27 – Short Reason Code Enhancements McLane host requires a user captured reason code for all inventory shortage during outbound order processing. For McLane, shorts occur during the wave process, during picking, and after the completion of picking. While WMOS supports reason code capture, it does not cover all touchpoints where MCLN users short inventory. Therefore, WMOS is customized to capture reason code for all shorting opportunities during outbound order processing. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Wave Shorts, Picking Short Items, oLPNs – Adjust Inventory, MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N McLane host requires that for any inventory shortage a reason code be captured by a user in the warehouse. During waving, base tracks inventory shortages through adjusted and canceled quantities. Since McLane requires a user to capture reason code for shortages, WMOS is customized to track shortages in the Wave Shorts UI and where users can assign a reason code for each order line shortage. During McLane picking process a user may short quantity that was allocated but not available to be picked thus creating Picking Short Item s records per base. When cancelling picking short item records base WMOS does not allow for reason code capture. Therefore, to aid in this WMOS enhances Picking Short Items UI to prompt for reason code capture when shorts are cancelled. Finally, since McLane systemically auto-completes packing for all their active picks, users cannot short during this process. Therefore, any shortages during physical packing need to be adjusted after the oLPNs have been systemically packed. WMOS in base allows users to adjust oLPN quantities through oLPN UI . However, in base oLPN – Adjust Inventory there is no reason code prompt. To allow McLane users to capture reason codes for oLPN adjustments, WMOS is enhanced with a reason code prompt in oLPN – Adjust Inventory UI . Assumptions Adjustment reason codes (B-051) are configured to cancel shortage (Parm 2-2 = Cancel Shortage Requirement) During wave on item shortage WMOS is configured for partial allocation. Assign Adjustment Reason Code UI is not supported for EX27 transactions Reason code capture in Wave Shorts UI is single capture and does not allow for re-capture. Communication of Reason Codes to host are handled through EX20 Ship Confirm Customizations If item is slotted to multiple pick locations, such as cigarette items, WMOS displays the location with no item attributes (unstamped) in Wave Shorts UI Wave Shorts UI displays total On Hand Quantity and does not consider allocated or locked quantities Reason codes are not populated in 620 level PIX as part of EX27 Chase wave is configured for partial allocation to allow for all picking short records to be manually cancelled by user Custom UI columns can be re-arranged and added or removed per base UI customize option Adjustments made through oLPNs UI only track a single reason code and for multiple adjustments for same detail the last updated reason code is saved Cancel wave is configured to adjust order line for shortages and create Wave Short records Picking Short Items for same order and item combination should be selected with the same reason code oLPN Cancel and adjust are handled as manual adjustments in both WM and Ecom system and no communication is expected for these actions. Ecom Picking Shortages are sent leveraging base 6120 and 6180 MHE messages. Users that attempt to cancel an oLPN in “Printed” status will receive “Invalid LPN status” message. In order for Ship Confirm to account for all lines, users need to follow one of the flows accounted for in this extension. Site 2 CR will bring this “Printed” oLPN status cancelation into acceptable flows. At that point, this assumption will be removed. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX20 Ship Confirm Customizations – Works in conjunction with EX20 to communicate reason codes to Host Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram Wave Process – Inventory Shortage Process Steps User navigates to Run Waves UI and runs wave During waving WMOS checks for inventory shortage . If shortage is found WMOS adjusts order line quantity for shortage and continues to next step. Else, WMOS continues with base wave processing for no shortage. WMOS updates custom table C_WAVE_SHORT with records for each Distribution Order Line that was adjusted with the shorted units. Wave Short UI Process Steps User navigates to Wave Shorts UI. User selects Wave Short record(s) and selects reason code button Wave Short Reason Code entry screen is shown to the user If user does not select reason code and selects confirm WMOS displays custom error message ‘No Reason Code Selected’. Else User selects cancel button and is returned to Wave shorts screen. If user selects reason code and selects confirm, WMOS continues with wave short processing. WMOS performs updates to wave short reason code updates for the selected wave short records Picking Short Items UI Process Steps User navigates to Picking Short Items UI. User selects Picking Short Items record(s) and selects cancel shorts WMOS displays Picking Short Items – Reason Code Screen If user does not select reason code and selects confirm WMOS displays custom error message ‘No Reason Code Selected’. Else User selects cancel button and is returned to Picking Short Items screen. If user selects reason code and selects confirm, WMOS continues with reason code processing. WMOS performs reason code updates to for picking short item record(s). oLPN Adjust Inventory Process Steps User navigates to oLPN UI. User selects oLPN record and selects Adjust Button. User selects the oLPN detail record to adjust, enters new quantity, then selects reason code. User clicks save to submit inventory adjustment. Enablement If system code C-000-000 and C-000-EX27 byte 1-1 are both enabled then the below custom validation is enabled. If system code C-X27-E27 byte 1-1 “EX27 Cancel Printed oLPN Error Message” =’Y’ then the below validation is enabled. If not, this validation is completely omitted, and base behavior continues. Validations A custom validation is added to ensure that users do not cancel oLPNs in “Printed” status. This is a flow outside of the SOPs which were designed for in this document and has caused issue. Until this flow is brought into SOP and fully designed around, this validation will force users to work within the designed for SOP. At the time of cancelation, WMOS will check the status of the oLPN which is being canceled. If the oLPN is in “Printed” status, then WMOS will throw base error message 1003 “Invalid LPN Status”. This will be a hard error and will not be able to be bypassed. Outputs PIX WMOS sends 620/01/05 or 620/01/06 per base when an oLPN is adjusted or cancelled through oLPNs UI. Process Change/Addition Wave Process – Inventory Shortage During McLane’s wave allocation process shortage can occur for order lines when no quantity or only part of the actual quantity is available for allocation. During this process, base WMOS shorts the order line and cancels remaining quantity per the ‘Adjust Order Line Quantity on Shortage’ configured on McLane’s wave parameters. Anytime this shortage process occurs within the wave this extension makes additional updates to populate custom table (C_WAVE_SHORT) with to track these shortages and their quantities. In addition, WMOS suppresses 620/01/05 and 620/01/06 PIX during the wave shortage processing. PIX for these shortages are then sent during Wave Short Record processing and include custom reason code. Alex Jones 2/12/2024 1.0 Updated distribution order display types to accurately reflect alphanumeric values EX27 – Short Reason Code Enhancements McLane host requires a user captured reason code for all inventory shortage during outbound order processing. For McLane, shorts occur during the wave process, during picking, and after the completion of picking. While WMOS supports reason code capture, it does not cover all touchpoints where MCLN users short inventory. Therefore, WMOS is customized to capture reason code for all shorting opportunities during outbound order processing. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Wave Shorts, Picking Short Items, oLPNs – Adjust Inventory, MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N McLane host requires that for any inventory shortage a reason code be captured by a user in the warehouse. During waving, base tracks inventory shortages through adjusted and canceled quantities. Since McLane requires a user to capture reason code for shortages, WMOS is customized to track shortages in the Wave Shorts UI and where users can assign a reason code for each order line shortage. During McLane picking process a user may short quantity that was allocated but not available to be picked thus creating Picking Short Item s records per base. When cancelling picking short item records base WMOS does not allow for reason code capture. Therefore, to aid in this WMOS enhances Picking Short Items UI to prompt for reason code capture when shorts are cancelled. Finally, since McLane systemically auto-completes packing for all their active picks, users cannot short during this process. Therefore, any shortages during physical packing need to be adjusted after the oLPNs have been systemically packed. WMOS in base allows users to adjust oLPN quantities through oLPN UI . However, in base oLPN – Adjust Inventory there is no reason code prompt. To allow McLane users to capture reason codes for oLPN adjustments, WMOS is enhanced with a reason code prompt in oLPN – Adjust Inventory UI . Assumptions Adjustment reason codes (B-051) are configured to cancel shortage (Parm 2-2 = Cancel Shortage Requirement) During wave on item shortage WMOS is configured for partial allocation.

Validations A custom validation is added to ensure that users do not cancel oLPNs in “Printed” status. This is a flow outside of the SOPs which were designed for in this document and has caused issue. Until this flow is brought into SOP and fully designed around, this validation will force users to work within the designed for SOP. At the time of cancelation, WMOS will check the status of the oLPN which is being canceled. If the oLPN is in “Printed” status, then WMOS will throw base error message 1003 “Invalid LPN Status”. This will be a hard error and will not be able to be bypassed. Outputs PIX WMOS sends 620/01/05 or 620/01/06 per base when an oLPN is adjusted or cancelled through oLPNs UI. Since base WMOS removes Order from Shipment and oLPN from Shipment during this action, WMOS is customized to fetch TC_SHIPMENT_ID and populate this field as reference field in 620/01/05 and 620/01/06 PIX. Process Change/Addition Wave Process – Inventory Shortage During McLane’s wave allocation process shortage can occur for order lines when no quantity or only part of the actual quantity is available for allocation. During this process, base WMOS shorts the order line and cancels remaining quantity per the ‘Adjust Order Line Quantity on Shortage’ configured on McLane’s wave parameters. Anytime this shortage process occurs within the wave this extension makes additional updates to populate custom table (C_WAVE_SHORT) with to track these shortages and their quantities. In addition, WMOS suppresses 620/01/05 and 620/01/06 PIX during the wave shortage processing. PIX for these shortages are then sent during Wave Short Record processing and include custom reason code. EX27 Close Shipment- Wave Shorts logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base close shipment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. Next, WMOS validates Wave Short records for the shipment selected for close.

If unprocessed Wave Short records exist for the shipment being closed, then WMOS displays custom error message CUST/2704 - “Unprocessed Wave Short records exist. Cannot close shipment .” Otherwise, WMOS continues to the base close shipment processing. Lastly, WMOS validates Picking Short records for the shipment selected for close.
